product: Okta Users API (Core Okta management API)
interface: Okta management REST API, path prefix /api/v1 (official OpenAPI spec 2026.07.1)

auth.oauth_alternative: Authorization: Bearer {access_token} with scope okta.users.manage (not exercised by these tests)

query.activate: false — REQUIRED here; the documented default is true, which would immediately activate

additional Universal Directory profile attributes pass through verbatim

request_body.note: no credentials object when the caller supplies none; activate is never a body field

success.body: User object: id, status=STAGED, created, activated (null until active), statusChanged, lastLogin, profile, credentials, _links

query.sendEmail: false — REQUIRED here; the documented default is true and emails the user

activate_user.preconditions: only valid for STAGED or DEPROVISIONED users; activation is asynchronous
activate_user.async_semantics: while the transition is in flight GET /api/v1/users/{userId} reports transitioningToStatus=ACTIVE; the property disappears and status becomes ACTIVE when the transition completes

hal_links.shape: _links is a HAL object: each relation maps to {href, method?}; the set of relations varies with user status (e.g. activate for STAGED, suspend/resetPassword once ACTIVE)
hal_links.requirement: clients must preserve every relation and href verbatim; hrefs are absolute URLs and must not be rebuilt

permission.errorSummary: You do not have permission to perform the requested action

transition.errorSummary: Activation failed because the user is already active

error_model.classification_rule: E0000006 and E0000016 are both HTTP 403 — classification must key on errorCode, not on status

mock_fixture.org_base_url: loopback http://127.0.0.1:{port} standing in for https://{yourOktaDomain}
mock_fixture.api_token: 00dummySSWSt0ken-fixture-only-91xQ (dummy; must never appear in exception messages)
